text.title: Characterization Test Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that separate working characterization suites from "tests I wrote and now ignore."

rule.statement: The full input fixture set MUST be captured and committed BEFORE any refactor commit lands. The refactor branch and the characterization-suite branch are NEVER the same — fixtures captured on the refactored code prove nothing.

rationale.source: Feathers, Working Effectively With Legacy Code Ch.6

rule.rationale: Capturing fixtures on already-refactored code recursively legitimizes whatever bugs the refactor introduced. The capture branch must reflect the pre-refactor production state, period.

rule.statement: Every snapshot/expected-output file MUST be reviewed line-by-line by a human (or by a deliberate second pass) the first time it is generated. "Auto-accept snapshot" is forbidden during initial capture. After the baseline is signed off, future updates require diff review and a commit message stating which behavior changed.
rule.rationale: Snapshot tests that are blindly accepted document whatever the code happened to do, including bugs. The reviewer step is what makes a characterization test a contract instead of a coincidence.

rule.statement: Non-deterministic outputs (timestamps, UUIDs, random IDs, ordering of unordered collections) MUST be normalized before snapshot comparison. The normalization rules are committed in a single file referenced by every snapshot-driven test. Ad-hoc per-test sanitization is forbidden.

rationale.source: Snapshot-testing failure analysis (Jest, syrupy, ApprovalTests issue trackers)

rule.rationale: Per-test sanitization rots immediately. A central rule file is the only sustainable way to keep the suite passing without false negatives.

rule.statement: The fixture sweep MUST have a documented termination criterion: e.g., branch coverage >= 90% on the target module, OR 100 production-sampled inputs exercised, OR every documented edge case in the spec has a fixture. "Until I get bored" is not a termination criterion.

rationale.source: Test-design discipline; without a stopping rule developers under-test critical paths and over-test trivial ones

rule.rationale: Termination criteria force the question "is this enough?" to be answered before the refactor starts, not after a regression is reported.

rule.statement: After the refactor, ANY snapshot diff is a behavior change that MUST be classified: (a) intentional fix (logged + retested), (b) accidental regression (revert), or (c) noise from new non-determinism (extend normalization rules). Snapshots are not auto-updated to "make CI green."

rationale.source: Working Effectively With Legacy Code Ch.7, plus practical post-mortems of failed legacy migrations

rule.rationale: Auto-updating snapshots is the most common failure of characterization testing in practice. It silently turns the suite into a no-op.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (legacy path callable, input samples reachable, snapshot library present), execute the full procedure: capture → normalize → snapshot → sweep-to-coverage-threshold → wrap-refactor. Do not skip the branch-coverage gate.
rule.rationale: Suites without a coverage gate become 5 happy-path tests; they pass the refactor and miss the long tail.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) the code is being deleted, (b) the refactor includes a bug fix and the current behavior is buggy — pinning a bug as expected is a trap, (c) the path already has strong unit tests — extend those, (d) no real input samples are reachable.
rule.rationale: Characterization-test overhead is wasted absent real input or behavior-preserving refactor intent.

example.title: Centralized normalization
example.code: # tests/fixtures/normalize.py def normalize(snapshot: dict) -> dict: snapshot = json.loads(json.dumps(snapshot, sort_keys=True)) snapshot["created_at"] = "<TS>" snapshot["request_id"] = "<UUID>" for item in snapshot.get("items", []): item.pop("computed_at_ms", None) return snapshot
example.note: One file. Every snapshot test imports it. Reviewer can audit the entire normalization surface in 20 lines.

example.title: Ad-hoc per-test sanitization
example.code: def test_quote(): result = legacy_quote(input_a) result.pop("created_at") # forgot in test_invoice result["id"] = "<ID>" # different placeholder than test_order assert result == snapshot
example.note: Two tests, two normalization schemes. The third will use a third. Within a quarter the suite is unmaintainable.
